feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Surprising ways menopause can affect your mouth
entry.summary: Hormone changes during menopause can mess with your dental and oral health. Here’s how experts recommend managing the effects.

entry.title: Scientists found the strength training sweet spot for a longer life
entry.summary: Just 90–120 minutes of strength training a week may deliver some of the biggest long-term health rewards, according to a study tracking more than 147,000 people for 30 years. That amount was linked to lower risks of death overall, particularly from cardiovasc…

entry.title: Popular joint pain supplement might increase Alzheimer's risk, study says
entry.summary: A popular over-the-counter supplement taken for joint pain might increase people's risk for Alzheimer's disease, a new study says.

entry.title: 'I've never been this good' – revolutionary immune reset puts lupus in remission
entry.summary: Patients on the trial have not needed medication to manage their condition.

entry.title: Meningitis B vaccine to be offered to thousands of young people in July
entry.summary: The decision for the one-off vaccine programme follows the unprecedented outbreak in Kent this year.

entry.title: Berkeley death from rat-linked infection serves as 'wake-up call,' expert says
entry.summary: On Wednesday, Berkeley city officials said a person died in May after contracting leptospirosis months after they issued a warning on the disease.

entry.title: Potential case of rare and deadly hantavirus investigated at Bay Area prison
entry.summary: Hantavirus, a "stealth infection," made recent headlines after an outbreak on a cruise ship.

entry.title: Collagen vs Hyaluronic Acid: Which Is Best For Anti-Aging?
entry.summary: The efficacy of collagen vs hyaluronic acid has long been debated among skincare experts. Vogue explores which of these hero ingredients is best for anti-aging.

entry.title: CDC and Florida at odds over hantavirus cruise ship passenger’s quarantine
entry.summary: Florida health officials are pushing back at quarantine guidance from the CDC for passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius exposed to the Andes hantavirus.

entry.title: New Antibiotic Found in Dirt Targets Superbugs in a Way ‘Never Been Seen Before’
entry.summary: Researchers tested their newly discovered peptide against one common antibiotic-resistant bacteria. Only about one in every hundred million survived to pass on genetic resistance.

entry.title: Deer, dog ticks are abundant in Maine right now. Here’s what to do about them.
entry.summary: Your frequently asked questions about ticks are answered

entry.title: Socioeconomic Status Leaves Deep Imprints on Developing Brains
entry.summary: A new study, analyzing 12,000 children proves socioeconomic factors drive 16% of brain development, completely outpacing IQ.

entry.title: Menopause hormone therapy: Demand rises but training gaps remain
entry.summary: As well as acute shortages in supply, there are other significant gaps in research and the training of health professionals.

entry.title: White House response to hantavirus and Ebola contrasts with COVID criticisms
entry.summary: The administration imposed mandatory quarantine orders on two passengers from the cruise ship hit by hantavirus and is blocking Americans who catch Ebola from returning home for treatment.

entry.title: There’s a Teenage Milestone That I Couldn’t Wait to Achieve. My Son Is Actively Avoiding It.
entry.summary: Every kid couldn't wait for this, I thought.

entry.title: Hypothalamic Histamine Waves Open and Close Memory Access
entry.summary: A new study proves spontaneous hypothalamic histamine fluctuations gate moment-to-moment memory accessibility.

entry.title: Can What You Eat Actually Help Prevent Dementia?
entry.summary: Experts explain whether what’s on your plate can make a meaningful difference in your dementia risk. Plus, healthy recipes to put it into practice.

entry.title: Genomic landscape of the human vaginal microbiome is linked to host genetics and population of origin
entry.summary: Metagenomics, coupled with genome-wide association analyses, highlights population-specific patterns of variation within the vaginal microbiome and identifies host genetic loci associated with vaginal microbiome composition.

entry.title: ‘Huge’ 8-Week-Old Golden Retriever Puppy Has the Entire Vet Office Obsessed
entry.summary: This precious puppy is popular.

entry.title: What’s the Future of Gene Editing?
entry.summary: In the first episode of the new season of ‘The Joy of Why,’ Nobel Laureate Jennifer Doudna discusses how she discovered CRISPR’s genome-editing power, the breakthroughs and hurdles during its explosive growth, and what lies ahead for this groundbreaking techn…

entry.title: A Small Part of Your Brain May Still Be Listening Under Anesthesia
entry.summary: Our brains might be more alert when unconscious than we realized.

entry.title: Waist Size and Muscle Mass: Predictors of Prognosis in CKD
entry.summary: In patients with CKD, lower muscle mass is linked to higher mortality and CKD progression, and higher visceral adiposity is linked to an increased risk for cardiovascular events, a recent study finds.

entry.title: A Very Contagious Virus Is on the Rise, but Doctors Say Doing This One Simple Thing Will Keep You Safe
entry.summary: Cases of norovirus, a.k.a. the "winter vomiting disease," are rising around the country known for serious symptoms. Doctors share why and how you can stay safe.

entry.title: The midlife habits that could make or break your brain health long-term
entry.summary: Researchers increasingly believe your 40s, 50s and 60s represent a critical window for protecting cognitive health later in life.

entry.title: Third rabies case confirmed in Wayne County in recent weeks
entry.summary: Rabies confirmed in a cat in Galen, NY, state lab says. One person exposed is being treated. Wayne County Public Health urges residents to avoid wildlife, vaccinate pets, and attend free rabies clinics this month.

entry.title: Physicists Discover How Slime Mold 'Makes Decisions' Without a Brain
entry.summary: Slime molds are slippery, nebulous beings.

entry.title: The internet’s favourite yoga instructor just shared a 10-minute routine to reset the nervous system, but what does that really mean and how can you benefit?
entry.summary: By practicing calming your body down, it can get better at switching off after periods of stress

entry.title: 4 'Bad' Vegetables You Should Eat for Weight Loss, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Some vegetables like corn and potatoes have earned a reputation that they’re bad for weight loss. Dietitians explain why that might not be true.

entry.title: Man with leptospirosis died after breeding rats in his RV, city says
entry.summary: A woman who lived with him got sick but recovered. The city said they were the first human cases of lepto in more than a decade.

entry.title: Anguished parents. Doctors in tears. Utah’s long measles outbreak takes a toll
entry.summary: SALT LAKE CITY (KFF Health News) — Ben Dowse hadn’t expected to treat measles when he became a doctor, but there he was, examining a newborn exposed to the virus in the womb. The infected mother had given birth just hours earlier. The hospital had alerted Dow…

entry.title: Person dies, another hospitalized for leptospirosis, Berkeley health officials say
entry.summary: The two people, who officials said were breeding rats in an RV, represent Berkeley's first known human cases of leptospirosis in a decade.

entry.title: Rare disease linked to rabbits has Colorado health officials on alert
entry.summary: Colorado health officials announced on June 9 that they had detected a rare disease linked to rabbits and ticks in the state.

entry.title: Sleep Experts Are Revealing The "Sweet Spot" Amount Of Sleep For Healthy Aging
entry.summary: Too little or too much sleep might be hurting you more than you think.

entry.title: Pennsylvania leaders say plunging vaccine rates in thousands of schools will be made public
entry.summary: False

entry.title: Mouse with Severed Spinal Cord Recovers ‘Normal’ Movement After Potentially Revolutionary Treatment
entry.summary: Biotech engineers in Zurich used micro-sized robots and stem cells to restore normal movement in a mouse whose spinal cord was cut.

entry.title: 71% Explosion in Social Anxiety Disorder
entry.summary: A new study tracks a 71% explosion in Social Anxiety Disorder, revealing that social phobia now affects 24% of young Canadians.

entry.title: The largest genetic study of anxiety symptom severity to date has found the condition is not only heritable but shows significant genetic overlap with heart disease, gut disorders, and migraine, in findings researchers describe as an important step forward in un
entry.summary: The question of whether anxiety is primarily a condition of the mind or of the body has never resolved cleanly, and a major new genetics study published yesterday does not resolve it either. What it does is sharpen the picture of how anxiety’s biological root…

entry.title: Decades-old puzzle solved as scientists uncover cause of inflammatory bowel disease
entry.summary: Researchers at the Nuffield Department of Medicine, University of Oxford, together with Newcastle University's Translational and Clinical Research Institute and the Department of Immunology at Cambridge University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust, have identifi…

entry.title: SIRT7 regulates dosage compensation and safeguards the female X chromosome
entry.summary: SIRT7 safeguards X-chromosome integrity and dosage balance with autosomes.

entry.title: Mutation-dependent responses to sleep and exercise in clonal haematopoiesis
entry.summary: Sleep and exercise can slow clonal haematopoiesis and limit mutant cell-driven atherosclerosis.

entry.title: Dietitian Explains What Really Happens When Coffee Hits An Empty Stomach First Thing In The Morning
entry.summary: While social media often portrays drinking coffee first thing in the morning as harmful, the reality is more nuanced and rooted in physiology.

entry.title: People Are Revealing The 20 Most Terrifying Scientific Facts, And I Wish I Could Unread These
entry.summary: Warning: Do NOT read any of these facts before bed tonight.

entry.title: No Lie, Scientists Have Developed a Fart Chart to Measure Gut Health. How Do You Rank?
entry.summary: New research breaks down how often people fart. Doctors say the data can be useful in the future.

entry.title: Childhood Trauma Timing Rewires Brain Fear Circuits
entry.summary: A new study links childhood trauma type and timing to altered fear brain circuits and IL-8/IL-17 inflammation.

entry.title: As the GLP-1 race heats up, how can investors position themselves for the next wave of innovation? (LLY:NYSE)
entry.summary: Track next-gen GLP-1 obesity & metabolic drugs—oral pills, triple agonists, combos—plus U.S. pricing and coverage impacts on Lilly and rivals.

entry.title: Simple 7-Day Meal Plan to Help Lower Your Dementia Risk, Created by a Dietitian
entry.summary: We incorporate the principles of the MIND diet in this simple 7-day meal plan for cognitive function, created by a dietitian.

entry.title: GLP-1 Drugs Reduce Depression, Boost Stress-Calming Gut Bacteria
entry.summary: A new study demonstrates GLP-1 drugs treat depression via gut Lactobacillus delbrueckii and endocannabinoids.

entry.title: OB-GYN group makes vaccine recommendations for the first time
entry.summary: A prominent OB-GYN group announced a set of vaccine recommendations on Wednesday that differs from what the U.S. government advises. The immunization schedule is specifically for pregnant, postpartum and breastfeeding women. It aligns with prior recommendatio…

entry.title: CRISPR enzyme precisely detects and shreds DNA in cancer mutations once considered 'undruggable'
entry.summary: In 2020, Jennifer Doudna won the Nobel Prize in chemistry for her work on the CRISPR-Cas9 gene-editing technology that allows scientists to precisely modify DNA by cutting it at specific locations. Six years later, a new study in Nature by a team led by Doudn…

entry.title: 5 Daily Exercises That Restore Muscle Tone Faster Than Gym Workouts After 60
entry.summary: A certified trainer, Terry Tateossian, shares 5 daily moves that restore muscle tone and strength after age 60.
